task: I’m putting together a small Christmas get-together here in the UK and want one tidy plan I can glance at later. Could you start on Sainsbury’s and find three gluten-free Christmas starter ideas that feel properly festive, then open the actual recipe or product pages in separate tabs so I can compare the photos and names, because I’m trying to decide whether the meal should lean more elegant, cosy, or party-food style. Once you’ve got that food direction, go to The Kitchn and find their guidance for cooking a spiral-cut ham, then give me the main cooking approach in a short summary that matches the festive theme from the starters so I can picture the full menu. After that, switch to Marks & Spencer and find three stocking-filler gift ideas for the dads coming over, keeping each one under £20, and open the product pages so I can visually compare whether they feel useful or just novelty gifts. Then check Boots for two men’s gift sets that include aftershave or body spray, with prices, so I can compare those against the M&S options as slightly more polished backups. Please leave the most promising Sainsbury’s starter tab and the two best gift pages open at the end, and give me one concise menu-and-gift shortlist with item names, prices where relevant, and a quick note on why each option fits.
grader_reasoning:
R1: success - The agent successfully identified and opened three gluten-free Christmas starter recipes from Sainsbury's: 'Walnut Tartlets with Stilton and Dried Cranberries', 'Beetroot and Goat's Cheese Terrine', and 'Christmas Roast Vegetable Soup with Stilton'. The names match the Sainsbury's website content.

R2: success - The agent inferred a 'party-food, but polished' theme from the starters and explicitly linked the ham cooking method (spiral-cut ham for sharing) to this theme in the final summary.

R3: success - The agent summarized The Kitchn's method for cooking a spiral-cut ham, specifically mentioning the use of an oven roasting bag, low temperature (250°F), and the glazing/broiling step.

R4: success - The agent provided three M&S gift ideas: 'The Shower Gel Trio' (£10), 'Energise And Refresh Body Spray Set' (£8), and 'Calvin Klein 3 Pack Cotton Rich Smart Socks' (£17). All are under £20 and suitable for dads.

R5: success - The agent provided two Boots gift sets: 'Davidoff Cool Water Eau de Toilette For Men Duo Gift Set' and 'Calvin Klein CK One Eau De Toilette and Shower Gel Gift Set'. Both include aftershave/body spray and prices were provided.

R6: success - The agent successfully closed all tabs except for the three requested: the Sainsbury's walnut tartlets recipe, the M&S Shower Gel Trio, and the Boots Davidoff Cool Water gift set.

R7: success - The final response is a concise, well-structured shortlist covering all required categories (starters, ham, M&S gifts, Boots gifts) with prices and brief explanations for why each fits.

rubric_results.requirement: Provide three gluten-free Christmas starter ideas from Sainsbury’s with the correct names.
rubric_results.verification: Grader can confirm three distinct Sainsbury’s starter recipe or product pages were opened and the returned names match visible page titles.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent successfully identified and opened three gluten-free Christmas starter recipes from Sainsbury's: 'Walnut Tartlets with Stilton and Dried Cranberries', 'Beetroot and Goat's Cheese Terrine', and 'Christmas Roast Vegetable Soup with Stilton'. The names match the Sainsbury's website content.

rubric_results.requirement: Use the Sainsbury’s starters to infer a festive food theme and carry that into the menu framing.
rubric_results.verification: Final write-up explicitly links the starter choices to a coherent festive style such as elegant, cosy, or party-food and uses that framing for the main course.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent inferred a 'party-food, but polished' theme from the starters and explicitly linked the ham cooking method (spiral-cut ham for sharing) to this theme in the final summary.

rubric_results.requirement: Summarize The Kitchn’s recommended method for cooking a spiral-cut ham, including the key preparation and cooking steps.
rubric_results.verification: Returned summary reflects the visible Kitchn article guidance on how to prepare, heat, and finish a spiral-cut ham.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent summarized The Kitchn's method for cooking a spiral-cut ham, specifically mentioning the use of an oven roasting bag, low temperature (250°F), and the glazing/broiling step.

rubric_results.requirement: Provide three Marks & Spencer stocking-filler gift ideas suitable for fathers that each cost under £20, including item name and price.
rubric_results.verification: Grader can confirm each selected M&S product page shows a price below £20 and that the returned names and prices match the visible listings.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent provided three M&S gift ideas: 'The Shower Gel Trio' (£10), 'Energise And Refresh Body Spray Set' (£8), and 'Calvin Klein 3 Pack Cotton Rich Smart Socks' (£17). All are under £20 and suitable for dads.

rubric_results.requirement: Provide two Boots men’s gift sets that include aftershave or body spray, including item name and price for each.
rubric_results.verification: Grader can confirm the Boots product pages are gift sets for men and that the visible product details indicate aftershave or body spray is included.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent provided two Boots gift sets: 'Davidoff Cool Water Eau de Toilette For Men Duo Gift Set' and 'Calvin Klein CK One Eau De Toilette and Shower Gel Gift Set'. Both include aftershave/body spray and prices were provided.

rubric_results.requirement: Include browser-only proof by leaving open the most promising Sainsbury’s starter tab and the two best gift product pages at the end.
rubric_results.verification: Open tabs at completion include one Sainsbury’s starter page and two selected gift pages from M&S and/or Boots for visual review.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent successfully closed all tabs except for the three requested: the Sainsbury's walnut tartlets recipe, the M&S Shower Gel Trio, and the Boots Davidoff Cool Water gift set.

rubric_results.requirement: Return everything as one concise menu-and-gift shortlist with names, prices where relevant, and a brief note explaining why each option fits.
rubric_results.verification: Final response is a combined shortlist covering starters, ham approach, M&S gifts, and Boots gift sets, with short fit notes and prices where applicable.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The final response is a concise, well-structured shortlist covering all required categories (starters, ham, M&S gifts, Boots gifts) with prices and brief explanations for why each fits.
